author: Yunhao Feng, Ruixiao Lin, Ming Wen, Qinqin He, Yanming Guo, Yifan Ding et al.

readme.entry: - **[Safety Testing LLM Agents at Scale: From Risk Discovery to Evidence-Grounded Verification](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.01793)** — Yunhao Feng, Ruixiao Lin, Ming Wen, Qinqin He, Yanming Guo, Yifan Ding et al. — <https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.01793> · *paper/benchmark/tool* — Vera/Vera-Bench turns agent safety risks into executable sandbox tests with deterministic evidence-grounded verifiers. 🆕

factuality.reason: structured evidence plus fetched source snapshot

quality.reason: concrete eval artifacts or methodology signals present

recency.reason: evidence indicates 2025-2026 or actively updated source

section: ## 10 · Safety / adversarial evaluation (prompt injection, jailbreaks, action-authorization, benchmark auditing)
source_seam: Hugging Face Daily Papers / arXiv primary read / GitHub repo
title: Safety Testing LLM Agents at Scale: From Risk Discovery to Evidence-Grounded Verification

worker.candidate_reason: Clears the bar because it releases a concrete framework and Vera-Bench: 1,600 executable safety cases across 124 risk categories, three execution settings, and four production agent frameworks, with outcomes judged from environment state and tool-call evidence rather than model self-report.
worker.evidence: HF read and arXiv page confirm v2 on 2026-07-04, 1,600 safety cases, 124 risk categories, OpenClaw/Hermes/Codex/Claude Code evaluation, 93.9% average multi-channel attack success, and code at https://github.com/Yunhao-Feng/Vera; GitHub README confirms evaluation_bench, deterministic verify.py, MCP gateway logs, and 12-container sandboxes.
worker.summary: Vera/Vera-Bench turns agent safety risks into executable sandbox tests with deterministic evidence-grounded verifiers.
